reasoning: Google Chrome has maintained an overwhelming >60% market share for many years. The knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, which is less than 2 months from the resolution date of January 1, 2026. Browser market share shifts extremely slowly, and a drop from >60% to below the next competitor (Safari at ~18%) in such a short window is practically impossible without a total global extinction of Chrome usage, which is not happening. I submit 0.95 as the maximum allowed confident probability.
